title: Telis caelesyriaca
taxonomy: Telis caelesyriaca is a species of fungus in the genus Telis, and it belongs to the family Pleosporaceae. This fungus is classified in the kingdom Fungi, phylum Ascomycota, and class Dothideomycetes. The order Pleosporales is the classificatory order for this species. Ascomycota is a division within the kingdom Fungi and is characterized by the presence of asci, which are spore-bearing structures. The classification of Telis caelesyriaca is an example of the complex and often evolving system used to categorize fungi.

intro: Telis caelesyriaca, also known as Syrian rue, is a flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating nausea, and exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Its bioactive constituents include alkaloids, particularly reserpine and serpentin, as well as other compounds like ajmalicine and akuammigine. Telis caelesyriaca is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments and support overall well-being.
